title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Horace Blatt and Patrick Redfern discuss local Devon legends regarding fairies and goblins. Redfern explains that folklore suggests goblins can lead people astray, though Blatt suggests such incidents are likely caused by intoxication. After Blatt leaves for dinner, Redfern tells Hercule Poirot that Blatt is a hardworking man obsessed with developing the area into a city.

When Poirot advises Redfern to avoid women to maintain a peaceful life, Redfern becomes angry, defending his marriage and blaming women like Mrs. Gardner and Mrs. Bruste for causing trouble. Later, Poirot finds Christine Redfern sitting outside in the damp evening. Christine expresses resentment toward being pitied as a "poor little woman." Poirot attempts to comfort her by discussing virtue and wisdom and assuring her of her husband's love, but Christine breaks down in tears and asks to be left alone.

As Poirot returns to the hotel, he discovers Elena Marshall and Patrick Redfern hiding in the bushes. He overhears Redfern professing his infatuation with Elena, who responds with soft, seemingly affectionate words. Poirot observes that Elena's expression appears self-satisfied. Shortly after, Elena approaches Poirot to make casual conversation about the weather.

On the morning of August 25th, Linda Marshall prepares for the day at the Pirate Flag Hotel. After a moment of intense reflection in her mirror, she dresses in a swimsuit and bathrobe. As she descends the stairs toward the sea, she encounters her father.
